_note: MOCK procedure result. No real patient. For PreChart development/eval. Result of the surveillance colonoscopy PT-011 was referred for.

procedure.name: Colonoscopy
procedure.indication: Surveillance colonoscopy (history of tubular adenomas with low-grade dysplasia, 2021; 5-year interval due 2026)
procedure.extent_reached: Cecum (confirmed by appendiceal orifice and ileocecal valve; terminal ileum intubated)

procedure_report: PROCEDURE: Colonoscopy. INDICATION: surveillance for prior tubular adenomas with low-grade dysplasia (2021), interval due 2026. PERI-PROCEDURE ANTICOAGULATION: Patient on warfarin 5 mg daily for atrial fibrillation (CHA2DS2-VASc 4). Warfarin held 5 days pre-procedure per anticoagulation clinic plan; INR checked morning of procedure = 1.2 (acceptable for polypectomy). Bridging with LMWH deferred given adequate INR normalization and endoscopic bleeding risk; resumption plan documented below. SEDATION: moderate. PREP: adequate (BBPS 8). FINDINGS: The colonoscope was advanced to the cecum and the terminal ileum was intubated and normal. A 7 mm sessile polyp was identified in the sigmoid colon and removed by cold snare polypectomy; specimen retrieved (specimen A). A 4 mm sessile polyp in the transverse colon was removed by cold snare polypectomy; specimen retrieved (specimen B). Scattered sigmoid diverticula were noted without stigmata of bleeding. No active bleeding at polypectomy sites; hemostasis was confirmed. The remainder of the examined colon was normal. IMPRESSION: 7 mm sigmoid polyp and 4 mm transverse polyp removed by cold snare; sigmoid diverticulosis. Pathology pending. Peri-procedure warfarin held with therapeutic reversal by INR; resumption instructions given.
disposition: Discharged home same day in stable condition with escort. Post-polypectomy precautions reviewed. Written anticoagulation resumption plan provided.

followup.recommendation: Review sigmoid and transverse polypectomy pathology; call patient with results and set surveillance interval
Resume warfarin the evening of the procedure (no immediate post-polypectomy bleeding); coordinate follow-up INR with anticoagulation clinic within 5-7 days
Counsel patient to report rectal bleeding, melena, or lightheadedness during the post-polypectomy window while re-anticoagulating

planted_followup_items.description: Sigmoid (7 mm) and transverse (4 mm) polypectomy pathology pending — results must be communicated to the patient and drive the surveillance interval
planted_followup_items.expected_action: call patient with results + document surveillance interval

planted_followup_items.description: Warfarin resumption after cold snare polypectomy in a high stroke-risk AF patient (CHA2DS2-VASc 4); warfarin was held 5 days pre-procedure
planted_followup_items.expected_action: confirm warfarin restarted evening of procedure and arrange follow-up INR with anticoagulation clinic within 5-7 days

planted_followup_items.description: Post-polypectomy delayed bleeding risk elevated by re-anticoagulation with warfarin
planted_followup_items.expected_action: ensure patient has bleeding precautions and knows when to seek care during 14-day post-polypectomy window

planted_followup_items.description: Surveillance interval to be finalized once pathology returns; prior tubular adenomas with LGD plus current polyps may shorten the next interval
planted_followup_items.expected_action: set and document next colonoscopy interval per pathology and polyp count

post_procedure_followup._note: Patient-reported stream after the procedure — the inbound signal the LeftBehind product triages. Each contact is labeled ground truth so triage can be scored. SYNTHETIC.

contacts.verbatim: My throat feels a bit scratchy and I'm all bloated and windy since yesterday.
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-endoscopy sore throat and insufflation-related bloating/flatus
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; mild throat irritation and gas/bloating are expected and typically resolve within 24-48h. Walking and warm fluids help.
contacts.rationale: Common benign post-colonoscopy symptoms with no red-flag features; no escalation.

contacts.verbatim: The nurse said I could start my warfarin again the night of the test, and I did. When will I hear back about the polyps, and is it okay that I'm back on the blood thinner already?
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient asking about pending pathology and confirming warfarin resumption timing; resumption was per plan (evening of procedure), no bleeding reported
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the result: pathology pending (expected ~2026-08-16), GI will call with results and surveillance interval; confirm warfarin resumption the evening of the procedure was correct per plan and remind her the anticoagulation clinic will recheck INR within 5-7 days. Review bleeding precautions.
contacts.rationale: Answerable from the procedure result and follow-up plan; resumption matches the documented med_resume plan, so this is reassurance/information rather than a med-timing error. Links to pending_pathology (F1).

contacts.verbatim: I saw a little streak of pink on the toilet paper once this morning. No dizziness, no real bleeding, everything else feels normal.
contacts.clinical_signal: Single episode of minor spotting after cold-snare polypectomy of small polyps; no recurrent bleeding, no clots, no hemodynamic symptoms, hemostasis confirmed intraprocedure
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; scant one-time spotting after small cold-snare polypectomy is common and self-limited. Advise return precautions: seek care for repeated or large-volume red blood, clots, black stools, lightheadedness, or fast/irregular heartbeat, especially while re-anticoagulated.
contacts.rationale: Triage trap — dangerous-sounding (bleeding on anticoagulation) but benign: a single trace of pink after a clean cold-snare exam with no volume, recurrence, or hemodynamic signal does not meet escalation criteria. Return precautions are reinforced against the F3 delayed-bleeding watch.
